cat > /tmp/sasl_files.txt << 'EOF'
# Core broker-side SASL authenticator
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java

# Authentication context lifecycle
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java

# Server callback handler
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java

# PLAIN mechanism server implementation
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServerProvider.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainServerCallbackHandler.java

# SCRAM mechanism server implementation  
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServerProvider.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramServerCallbackHandler.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java

# OAUTHBEARER mechanism server implementation
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider.java

# GSSAPI/Kerberos server callback handler
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosClientCallbackHandler.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosLogin.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosName.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/kerberos/KerberosShortNamer.java

# Supporting SASL infrastructure
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/SaslConfigs.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/internals/BrokerSecurityConfigs.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslExtensions.java
clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.java
EOF
cat /tmp/sasl_files.txt

    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.java", "symbol": "build"}
  ],
  "text": "Apache Kafka implements SASL broker-side authentication through a layered architecture. The core broker-side SASL authenticator is SaslServerAuthenticator (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java), which implements the Authenticator interface and manages the entire authentication flow on the broker side. It defines a SaslState enum to track authentication state transitions (INITIAL_REQUEST -> HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST -> HANDSHAKE_REQUEST -> AUTHENTICATE -> COMPLETE or FAILED). The authenticate() method is the main entry point that orchestrates the authentication process. Mechanism selection occurs in handleKafkaRequest() which parses incoming Kafka requests, and handleHandshakeRequest() which validates the client-requested mechanism against the broker's enabled mechanisms (configured in BrokerSecurityConfigs). When a supported mechanism is selected, createSaslServer() instantiates the appropriate SaslServer implementation based on the mechanism type. For GSSAPI, it uses special handling via createSaslKerberosServer() to create a Kerberos-authenticated subject. The broker supports four SASL mechanisms: PLAIN (PlainSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java), SCRAM (ScramSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java), GSSAPI/Kerberos (using Java's built-in mechanism with KerberosClientCallbackHandler), and OAUTHBEARER (OAuthBearerSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java). Each mechanism has a corresponding SaslServer implementation, SaslServerProvider factory, and CallbackHandler. SCRAM mechanisms (SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512) are defined by ScramMechanism in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java. The authentication context lifecycle is managed by SaslAuthenticationContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java), which encapsulates the SaslServer instance, security protocol, client address, and listener name. This context is created after successful mechanism negotiation and passed through the authentication pipeline. Token exchange occurs via evaluateResponse() on each mechanism's SaslServer implementation, with responses being sent back through the transport layer. After authentication succeeds, the SaslServer provides the authenticated principal through getAuthorizationID(), which DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.java) uses to build a KafkaPrincipal. For GSSAPI, KerberosShortNamer is applied to transform the principal name. The SaslServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java) handles callbacks during authentication, dispatching to mechanism-specific handlers like PlainServerCallbackHandler and ScramServerCallbackHandler for credential validation. SaslExtensions allow per-mechanism extensions to the authentication protocol."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kafka SASL Authentication Mechanism Implementation

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in apache/kafka that implement SASL broker-side authentication: the SASL server authenticator, the mechanism selection (PLAIN, GSSAPI, SCRAM), and the authentication context lifecycle.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Java source files implementing SASL broker-side authentication in Apache Kafka. Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Apache Kafka SASL broker-side authentication implementation and created the answer file at `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary of Findings

I've identified all Java source files implementing SASL broker-side authentication in Apache Kafka:

**Core Components:**
1. **Main SASL Server Authenticator**: `SaslServerAuthenticator` - orchestrates the entire broker-side authentication flow with state machine (INITIAL_REQUEST → HANDSHAKE → AUTHENTICATE → COMPLETE/FAILED)

2. **Mechanism Selection**: Handled through:
   - `handleKafkaRequest()` - parses incoming requests
   - `handleHandshakeRequest()` - validates client mechanism against enabled mechanisms
   - `createSaslServer()` - instantiates appropriate mechanism implementation

3. **Mechanism Implementations** (4 supported):
   - **PLAIN**: `PlainSaslServer` with `PlainSaslServerProvider` and `PlainServerCallbackHandler`
   - **SCRAM**: `ScramSaslServer` with `ScramSaslServerProvider` and `ScramServerCallbackHandler`
   - **GSSAPI/Kerberos**: Uses Java's built-in SASL mechanism with `KerberosClientCallbackHandler`
   - **OAUTHBEARER**: `OAuthBearerSaslServer` with `OAuthBearerSaslServerProvider`

4. **Authentication Context Lifecycle**: `SaslAuthenticationContext` encapsulates the SaslServer instance, security protocol, client address, and listener name

5. **Supporting Infrastructure**: Configuration via `SaslConfigs`, broker security configs, principal building via `DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder`

The answer file includes 20 key source files, 16 important symbols, and a 9-step authentication chain that shows the flow from initial authentication request through mechanism negotiation to credential validation.

    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java", "symbol": "evaluateResponse"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java", "symbol": "evaluateResponse"},

  "text": "Apache Kafka implements SASL broker-side authentication through a layered architecture. The core broker-side SASL authenticator is SaslServerAuthenticator (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator.java), which implements the Authenticator interface and manages the entire authentication flow on the broker side. It defines a SaslState enum to track authentication state transitions (INITIAL_REQUEST -> HANDSHAKE_OR_VERSIONS_REQUEST -> HANDSHAKE_REQUEST -> AUTHENTICATE -> COMPLETE or FAILED). The authenticate() method is the main entry point that orchestrates the authentication process. Mechanism selection occurs in handleKafkaRequest() which parses incoming Kafka requests, and handleHandshakeRequest() which validates the client-requested mechanism against the broker's enabled mechanisms (configured in BrokerSecurityConfigs). When a supported mechanism is selected, createSaslServer() instantiates the appropriate SaslServer implementation based on the mechanism type. For GSSAPI, it uses special handling via createSaslKerberosServer() to create a Kerberos-authenticated subject. The broker supports four SASL mechanisms: PLAIN (PlainSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/plain/internals/PlainSaslServer.java), SCRAM (ScramSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramSaslServer.java), GSSAPI/Kerberos (using Java's built-in mechanism with KerberosClientCallbackHandler), and OAUTHBEARER (OAuthBearerSaslServer in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/oauthbearer/internals/OAuthBearerSaslServer.java). Each mechanism has a corresponding SaslServer implementation, SaslServerProvider factory, and CallbackHandler. SCRAM mechanisms (SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512) are defined by ScramMechanism in clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/scram/internals/ScramMechanism.java. The authentication context lifecycle is managed by SaslAuthenticationContext (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/auth/SaslAuthenticationContext.java), which encapsulates the SaslServer instance, security protocol, client address, and listener name. This context is created after successful mechanism negotiation and passed through the authentication pipeline. Token exchange occurs via evaluateResponse() on each mechanism's SaslServer implementation, with responses being sent back through the transport layer. After authentication succeeds, the SaslServer provides the authenticated principal through getAuthorizationID(), which DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/DefaultKafkaPrincipalBuilder.java) uses to build a KafkaPrincipal. For GSSAPI, KerberosShortNamer is applied to transform the principal name. The SaslServerCallbackHandler (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/security/authenticator/SaslServerCallbackHandler.java) handles callbacks during authentication, dispatching to mechanism-specific handlers like PlainServerCallbackHandler and ScramServerCallbackHandler for credential validation. SaslExtensions allow per-mechanism extensions to the authentication protocol."
}
